_meta.description: Apolytikia (troparia) and kontakia for movable feasts and major fixed-cycle saints. Same dual-key shape as lectionary: 'movable' keyed by pdist, 'fixed' keyed by canonical Menaion MM-DD.
_meta.license: Texts are original English translations of public-domain Greek/Slavonic hymnody. Theosis owns these English renderings.
_meta.coverage: Major movable feasts (Palm Sunday, Pascha, Ascension, Pentecost, All Saints) plus principal May fixed feasts. Long tail of saints/feasts populated incrementally.

-7.title: Troparion of Palm Sunday
-7.text: Confirming the universal Resurrection before Thy Passion, Thou didst raise Lazarus from the dead, O Christ our God. Therefore, like the children, we also bear emblems of victory and cry to Thee, the Conqueror of death: Hosanna in the highest, blessed is He that cometh in the name of the Lord.

-7.title: Kontakion of Palm Sunday
-7.text: Borne upon a throne in heaven, and upon a foal upon the earth, O Christ our God, Thou didst accept the praise of angels and the hymn of children crying out: Blessed art Thou that comest to call back Adam.

-2.title: Troparion of Holy Friday
-2.text: The noble Joseph, taking Thy most pure Body down from the Tree, wrapped it in clean linen with sweet spices and laid it in a new tomb. But on the third day Thou didst rise, O Lord, granting the world great mercy.

0.title: Paschal Troparion
0.text: Christ is risen from the dead, trampling down death by death, and to those in the tombs bestowing life.

0.title: Paschal Kontakion
0.text: Though Thou didst descend into the grave, O Immortal One, yet didst Thou destroy the power of Hades, and didst rise as a victor, O Christ our God; Thou didst greet the myrrh-bearing women saying, Rejoice! and grant peace unto Thine apostles, Thou who givest resurrection to the fallen.

7.title: Troparion of Thomas Sunday
7.text: While the tomb was sealed, Thou, O Life, didst shine forth from the grave, O Christ God; and when the doors were shut, Thou didst come unto Thy disciples, the Resurrection of all, renewing through them an upright Spirit in us, according to Thy great mercy.

21.title: Kontakion of the Paralytic
21.text: By Thy divine intervention, O Lord, raise up my soul which is grievously paralyzed by sins of every kind and by unseemly deeds, even as Thou didst of old raise up the paralytic, that being saved I may cry unto Thee: Glory to Thy power, O compassionate Christ.

24.title: Troparion of Mid-Pentecost
24.text: At the midpoint of the feast, O Saviour, water my thirsty soul with the streams of true piety, for to all Thou didst cry out: Whosoever thirsteth, let him come to Me and drink. O Christ God, the Wellspring of life, glory to Thee.

28.title: Kontakion of the Samaritan Woman
28.text: The Samaritan woman, having come in faith unto the well, beheld Thee, the Water of Wisdom. Drinking thereof abundantly, she inherited the Kingdom on high; therefore, she is forever exalted.

39.title: Troparion of the Ascension
39.text: Thou hast ascended in glory, O Christ our God, granting joy to Thy disciples by the promise of the Holy Spirit. Through the blessing they were assured that Thou art the Son of God, the Redeemer of the world.

39.title: Kontakion of the Ascension
39.text: When Thou hadst fulfilled the dispensation for our sake, and united things on earth with the things in heaven, Thou didst ascend in glory, O Christ our God, in no wise departing from us, but remaining inseparable, and crying unto those who love Thee: I am with you, and there is none against you.

49.title: Troparion of Pentecost
49.text: Blessed art Thou, O Christ our God, who hast made the fishermen wise, by sending down upon them the Holy Spirit, and through them drawing the world into Thy net. O Lover of mankind, glory to Thee.

49.title: Kontakion of Pentecost
49.text: When the Most High came down and confused the tongues, He divided the nations; but when He distributed the tongues of fire, He called all to unity. With one accord, therefore, let us glorify the all-Holy Spirit.

56.title: Troparion of All Saints
56.text: Adorned in the blood of Thy martyrs throughout all the world as in purple and fine linen, Thy Church, through them, doth cry unto Thee, O Christ God: send down Thy compassion upon Thy people; grant peace to Thy commonwealth, and to our souls great mercy.

56.title: Kontakion of All Saints
56.text: To Thee, O Lord, the Author of creation, the world doth offer the God-bearing martyrs as first-fruits of nature. By their entreaties keep Thy Church, Thine own commonwealth, in deep peace, through the Theotokos, O Thou greatly merciful.

05-02.title: Troparion of St. Athanasius the Great
05-02.text: O thou who art a pillar of Orthodoxy, who didst confirm the Church through divine doctrines, hierarch Athanasius, thou hast been shown forth to be a sage of God; for by proclaiming the Son co-essential with the Father, thou didst put to shame the impious Arius. O righteous father, pray to Christ God to grant great mercy unto us.

05-08.title: Troparion of the Apostle and Evangelist John
05-08.text: O Beloved Apostle of Christ our God, hasten to deliver thy defenseless people. He upon whose breast thou didst lean accepteth thee as thou kneelest before Him. Beseech Him, O Theologian, to scatter the persisting cloud of the heathen and ask for us peace and great mercy.

05-08.title: Kontakion of the Apostle and Evangelist John
05-08.text: Who can recount thy mighty works, O Virgin Apostle? For thou pourest forth miracles and effusions of healings, and prayest for our souls as Theologian and Friend of Christ.

05-09.title: Troparion of the Translation of the Relics of St. Nicholas
05-09.text: The day of bright festival is come; the city of Bari rejoiceth, and with her the whole world doth exult in hymns and psalms. For today is the sacred feast of the translation of the precious and much-healing relics of the hierarch Nicholas the wonderworker. Like a never-setting sun, having risen, he illumineth the faithful with the brilliance of his miracles.

05-11.title: Troparion of Sts. Cyril and Methodius
05-11.text: O holy Equals-to-the-Apostles, illumined of the Slavic lands, fathers Cyril and Methodius wise in God: entreat the Master of all to confirm all Orthodox peoples in the true Faith and concord, granting peace unto the world and great mercy to our souls.

05-15.title: Troparion of St. Pachomius the Great
05-15.text: Thou didst prove to be a citizen of the desert, an angel in the flesh, and a wonderworker, O Pachomius our God-bearing father. Having received gifts from heaven through fasting, vigil, and prayer, thou didst heal the sick and the souls of those who turned to thee in faith. Glory to Him who gave thee strength; glory to Him who crowned thee; glory to Him who through thee worketh healings for all.

05-21.title: Troparion of Sts. Constantine and Helen
05-21.text: Having seen the image of Thy Cross in heaven, and like Paul having received the call not from men, Thine apostle among kings entrusted the imperial city to Thy hand. Save us always in peace, through the prayers of the Theotokos, O only Lover of mankind.

05-21.title: Kontakion of Sts. Constantine and Helen
05-21.text: Today Constantine and his mother Helen reveal the Cross, the all-precious wood. It is a shame to all the Jews, and a weapon of faithful kings against the adversary. For our sake hath appeared this mighty sign and awesome in war.

05-25.title: Troparion of the Third Finding of the Head of the Forerunner
05-25.text: The Forerunner's sacred head, having dawned forth from the earth, doth send forth incorrupt rays of healing to the faithful. From on high it gathereth the choir of angels, and from below it summoneth the company of mankind to send up with one voice glory unto Christ God.

06-24.title: Troparion of the Nativity of the Forerunner
06-24.text: O Prophet and Forerunner of the coming of Christ, though we honor thee with love, we cannot worthily extol thee, for thy mother's barrenness and thy father's voicelessness were undone by thy honored and glorious nativity, and the Incarnation of the Son of God was proclaimed to the world.

06-29.title: Troparion of the Holy Apostles Peter and Paul
06-29.text: O first-throned of the Apostles and teachers of the world, intercede with the Master of all that He may grant peace to the world and to our souls great mercy.

06-29.title: Kontakion of the Holy Apostles Peter and Paul
06-29.text: O Lord, Thou hast received the steadfast and divinely-inspired heralds, the chief among Thine Apostles, into the enjoyment of Thy good things and into Thy rest, for Thou hast accepted their labors and their death as above every burnt offering, O Thou who alone knowest the hearts of men.

07-20.title: Troparion of the Prophet Elijah
07-20.text: O angelic incarnate prophet, foundation of prophets, the second forerunner of the coming of Christ, glorious Elijah, who from on high didst send down grace upon Elisha to dispel diseases and cleanse lepers, for those who honor thee thou pourest forth abundant healings.

07-22.title: Troparion of St. Mary Magdalene
07-22.text: Christ, who was born of the Virgin for our sake, did Thou deliver from Whom didst expel seven demons, holy Mary Magdalene. Thou didst become the first to behold the divine Resurrection, and as the equal-to-the-Apostles, thou didst preach Him risen from the dead unto all the ends of the earth.

08-06.title: Troparion of the Transfiguration
08-06.text: Thou wast transfigured on the mount, O Christ God, revealing Thy glory to Thy disciples as far as they could bear it. Let Thine everlasting light shine also upon us sinners, through the prayers of the Theotokos. O Giver of light, glory to Thee.

08-06.title: Kontakion of the Transfiguration
08-06.text: On the mountain wast Thou transfigured, O Christ God, and Thy disciples beheld Thy glory as far as they could bear it, so that when they would behold Thee crucified they would understand Thy voluntary Passion, and proclaim to the world that Thou art truly the radiance of the Father.

08-15.title: Troparion of the Dormition
08-15.text: In giving birth thou didst preserve thy virginity; in thy dormition thou didst not forsake the world, O Theotokos. Thou wast translated unto life, since thou art the Mother of Life, and by thine intercessions thou dost deliver our souls from death.

08-15.title: Kontakion of the Dormition
08-15.text: Neither the tomb nor death had power over the Theotokos, who is sleepless in her intercessions and unfailing hope in her protection; for as the Mother of Life she hath been translated unto life by Him who dwelt in her ever-virgin womb.

08-29.title: Troparion of the Beheading of the Forerunner
08-29.text: The just man is remembered with songs of praise, but for thee the testimony of the Lord is sufficient, O Forerunner. Truly thou wast revealed more honorable than the prophets, for thou wast deemed worthy to baptize in the Jordan Him whom they had preached. Therefore having contested for the truth, thou didst rejoice to bring the good tidings even to those in hell — that God hath appeared in the flesh, taking away the sin of the world and granting us great mercy.

09-08.title: Troparion of the Nativity of the Theotokos
09-08.text: Thy nativity, O Theotokos, has proclaimed joy to the whole universe: for from thee did shine forth the Sun of Righteousness, Christ our God. He hath loosed the curse and granted the blessing; He hath overcome death and bestowed on us eternal life.

09-08.title: Kontakion of the Nativity of the Theotokos
09-08.text: By thy nativity, O most pure Virgin, Joachim and Anna were freed from the reproach of barrenness, and Adam and Eve from the corruption of death. Thy people, redeemed from the guilt of their sins, exult and cry to thee: 'The barren one bringeth forth the Theotokos and the nourisher of our Life.'

09-14.title: Troparion of the Exaltation of the Cross
09-14.text: O Lord, save Thy people and bless Thine inheritance. Grant victory to the Orthodox Christians over their adversaries, and by the power of Thy Cross, preserve Thy commonwealth.

09-14.title: Kontakion of the Exaltation of the Cross
09-14.text: O Christ our God, Thou wast lifted up upon the Cross of Thine own will. Therefore grant Thy mercies unto Thy new commonwealth which is called by Thy name. By Thy power gladden the Orthodox Christians, and grant them victory over every adversary, having as an ally that weapon of peace, the trophy invincible.

11-08.title: Troparion of the Bodiless Powers
11-08.text: O Captains of the heavenly armies, we who are unworthy ever beseech you to deliver us by your supplications from terrors and to cover us with the protection of the wings of your immaterial glory, even as we fall down and cry to you: deliver us from danger, O captains of the angelic hosts.

11-21.title: Troparion of the Entrance of the Theotokos
11-21.text: Today is the prelude of God's good will and the proclamation of mankind's salvation. The Virgin appeareth openly in the temple of God, and foretelleth Christ to all. Unto her, then, let us also cry aloud: Rejoice, O fulfillment of the Creator's counsel.

11-21.title: Kontakion of the Entrance of the Theotokos
11-21.text: The most pure temple of the Saviour, the very precious bridal chamber and Virgin, the holy treasure of the glory of God, is led on this day into the house of the Lord, bringing with her the grace of the Divine Spirit. Of her doth the angels of God sing: 'Behold the heavenly tabernacle.'

11-30.title: Troparion of the First-Called Apostle Andrew
11-30.text: O Apostle Andrew, first-called of all the Twelve and brother of him who was foremost of the chief Apostles, beseech the Master of all to grant peace to the world and to our souls great mercy.

12-06.title: Troparion of St. Nicholas
12-06.text: The truth of things hath revealed thee to thy flock as a rule of faith, an icon of meekness, and a teacher of temperance; for which cause thou hast achieved the heights by humility, riches by poverty. O Father and Hierarch Nicholas, intercede with Christ God that our souls may be saved.

12-06.title: Kontakion of St. Nicholas
12-06.text: Thou wast a true minister at Myra, O Saint Nicholas, and didst fulfill Christ's Gospel, laying down thy life for thy people and saving the innocent from death. Therefore thou hast been sanctified, as a great minister of the grace of God.

12-25.title: Troparion of the Nativity of Christ
12-25.text: Thy Nativity, O Christ our God, hath shone forth upon the world the light of knowledge; for thereby they that worshipped the stars were taught by a star to worship Thee, the Sun of Righteousness, and to know Thee, the Dayspring from on high. O Lord, glory to Thee.

12-25.title: Kontakion of the Nativity of Christ
12-25.text: Today the Virgin giveth birth to Him who is transcendent in essence, and the earth offereth a cave to Him who is unapproachable. Angels with shepherds give glory, and Magi journey with a star, for to us is born a young Child, the pre-eternal God.

12-27.title: Troparion of the Protomartyr Stephen
12-27.text: O Protomartyr and servant of Christ, thou didst conquer in struggle and didst overthrow the wickedness of the enemies. As the first to be crowned, glorious Stephen, thou prayest now to Christ for the salvation of our souls.

01-01.title: Troparion of the Circumcision
01-01.text: Without leaving the bosom of the Father, the Lord of all hath come down upon the earth, and from a holy Virgin, having put on our flesh and becoming subject to the Law, was circumcised on the eighth day. Wherefore Saint Basil, who in this day was named by thee, doth join us in praising thee.

01-01.title: Troparion of St. Basil the Great
01-01.text: Thy proclamation hath gone forth into all the earth, which was divinely taught by hearing thy voice. Thou didst expound the nature of creatures, ennoble the manners of men, and royal priesthood, O righteous Father. Pray to Christ God to save our souls.

01-06.title: Troparion of the Theophany
01-06.text: When Thou, O Lord, wast baptized in the Jordan, the worship of the Trinity was made manifest, for the voice of the Father gave witness to Thee, calling Thee His beloved Son. And the Spirit, in the form of a dove, confirmed the truthfulness of His word. O Christ our God, who hast appeared and enlightened the world, glory to Thee.

01-06.title: Kontakion of the Theophany
01-06.text: On this day Thou hast appeared unto the whole world, and Thy light, O Lord, hath been signed upon us, who with knowledge sing Thy praises: Thou hast come and revealed Thyself, the Light Unapproachable.

01-17.title: Troparion of St. Anthony the Great
01-17.text: Emulating the ways of Elijah the zealot, and following the straight paths of John the Baptist, O Father Anthony, thou didst become a dweller of the wilderness and didst confirm the world by thy prayers. Wherefore intercede with Christ God that our souls be saved.

01-25.title: Troparion of St. Gregory the Theologian
01-25.text: The pastoral pipe of thy theology overcame the trumpets of the rhetoricians, for the depths of the Spirit having been searched out by thee, the beauty of the word was added to thee. Intercede with Christ God, O Father Gregory, that our souls be saved.

01-30.title: Troparion of the Three Hierarchs
01-30.text: As the Apostles equal in honor and the teachers of the world, intercede with the Master of all to grant peace to the world and to our souls great mercy.

01-30.title: Kontakion of the Three Hierarchs
01-30.text: Thou hast taken to Thyself, O Lord, the divinely-eloquent and sacred heralds, the foremost teachers, into the enjoyment of Thy good things and into Thy rest: for Thou hast accepted their labors and their death above every offering, O Thou who alone glorifiest Thy saints.

02-02.title: Troparion of the Meeting of the Lord
02-02.text: Rejoice, O Virgin Theotokos, full of grace, for from thee hath risen the Sun of Righteousness, Christ our God, enlightening those who sat in darkness. Be glad also, O righteous Elder, for thou didst bear in thine arms the Liberator of our souls, who granteth us also the resurrection.

02-02.title: Kontakion of the Meeting of the Lord
02-02.text: Thou who by Thy birth didst sanctify the womb of the Virgin, and who didst bless the hands of Simeon as was meet, by anticipation hast even now saved us, O Christ God. But give peace in the midst of wars unto Thy commonwealth, and strengthen the rulers whom Thou hast loved, O only Lover of mankind.

03-09.title: Troparion of the Forty Martyrs
03-09.text: Through the sufferings which Thy holy Forty Martyrs endured for Thy sake, O Lord, we beseech Thee, O Lover of mankind: heal all our infirmities.

03-25.title: Troparion of the Annunciation
03-25.text: Today is the beginning of our salvation, the revelation of the eternal mystery! The Son of God becomes the Son of the Virgin as Gabriel announces the coming of Grace. Together with him let us cry to the Theotokos: Rejoice, O Full of Grace, the Lord is with thee!

03-25.title: Kontakion of the Annunciation
03-25.text: To thee the Champion Leader we, thy servants, dedicate a feast of victory and thanksgiving as ones rescued out of sufferings, O Theotokos: but as thou art one with might which is invincible, from all dangers that can be, do thou deliver us, that we may cry unto thee: Rejoice, O Bride unwedded.

04-23.title: Troparion of Great-martyr George
04-23.text: As the deliverer of captives and the protector of the poor, as the healer of the sick and the champion of kings, victorious great-martyr George, intercede with Christ God for the salvation of our souls.

04-25.title: Troparion of the Apostle and Evangelist Mark
04-25.text: Thou wast the disciple and companion of the chief of the Apostles; in proclaiming the Gospel, thou didst illumine Egypt with the light of the knowledge of God. O Apostle and Evangelist Mark, intercede with Christ God to grant great mercy unto our souls.
